notes: TPS realistic = sustained observed in production (block explorers / public benchmarks). TPS theoretical = max in whitepaper / docs. Where ambiguous, lower bound is used. OmniBus has NO mainnet — testnet/devnet only. | Updated 2026-04-25: added LCX Liberty Chain + Base (Coinbase L2)

chains.name: OmniBus

performance.tps_note: No public benchmark; design target is sub-second finality via 10x100ms sub-blocks. No mainnet to measure.

crypto.pq_status: Native, integrated via liboqs (CRYSTALS NIST PQC standards)

economics.tx_cost_note: Mempool-pressure based fee 1-100 SAT; no live market for OMNI

ecosystem.audit_status: No external audit. Bare-metal layer 5 of OmniBus OS uses Ada SPARK in places.

highlights: Native PQ crypto from day 0 (4 NIST PQC schemes via liboqs)
Sub-blocks (10 x 100ms) targeting sub-second finality
Bitcoin-compatible secp256k1 signatures + ECDSA addresses
Sharding planned (4 shards in design)
Integrated with bare-metal OmniBus OS layer 5 (BlockchainOS at 0x250000)
weaknesses: NO MAINNET — testnet/devnet only as of 2025-Q1
Single dev team, no external auditors
No general-purpose VM (no smart contracts beyond native ops)
Zero TVL, zero users, zero economic security
PoUW consensus unproven at scale (no published benchmark TPS)

chains.name: Bitcoin

performance.tps_note: ~4-7 tx/s base layer; Lightning Network adds off-chain capacity

crypto.pq_status: Not PQ. Reused-address P2PKH exposes pubkey; Shor's would break ECDSA

ecosystem.audit_status: Most-reviewed codebase in crypto; no formal verification of Core

highlights: Highest economic security (~600 EH/s hashrate, $1T+ market cap)
Longest live track record (16+ years uninterrupted)
Hardest money: 21M cap, predictable issuance, halvings
Lightning Network L2 for instant micropayments
Taproot/Schnorr aggregation improves privacy + scalability
weaknesses: ~4-7 TPS base layer is unsuitable for high-volume use
Not quantum-resistant; ~4M coins in vulnerable P2PK addresses
No native smart contracts beyond Script

chains.name: Ethereum

performance.tps_note: L1 only ~15 TPS. With L2 rollups (Arbitrum/Optimism/Base) aggregate is much higher.

architecture.sharding_note: Danksharding planned; current scaling via blob-carrying tx (EIP-4844) for rollups

crypto.pq_status: Not PQ. Vitalik proposed PQ migration via account abstraction (long-term roadmap)

economics.max_supply: unlimited (slightly deflationary post-EIP-1559 + Merge under high usage)
economics.block_reward: ~0 issuance to PoS validators net of burn under high gas
economics.tx_cost_note: Highly variable (0.50-50 USD); EIP-1559 base fee + priority

ecosystem.audit_status: Heavily audited; consensus specs formally specified (executable Python + spec tests)

highlights: Largest smart-contract ecosystem ($60B+ TVL)
Successful Merge to PoS (Sep 2022) cut energy ~99.95%
EIP-4844 blobs make L2 rollups affordable
Account abstraction (ERC-4337) live on mainnet
Most decentralized validator set (~1M validators)
weaknesses: ~15 TPS L1 is a bottleneck without rollups
Gas fees spike to $20-50+ during NFT/DeFi peaks
12-min finality is slow vs. modern chains

chains.name: MultiversX (EGLD)

performance.tps_note: Cross-shard 263k peak in stress test (3 shards x metachain). Steady-state production ~hundreds.

architecture.consensus: Secure PoS (SPoS) — random validator selection per round

highlights: Adaptive state sharding (3 shards + metachain)
Sub-second cross-shard finality in same epoch
Native ESDT tokens (no ERC-20 contracts needed)
Cheap fees (~$0.001) with 6s blocks
weaknesses: 263k TPS figure is stress-test peak, not sustained
Smaller dev ecosystem vs. EVM chains
WASM VM less tooling than EVM

chains.name: Solana

performance.tps_note: Whitepaper claims 65k. On-chain non-vote TPS averages ~1k-3k. Including vote tx ~3k-5k. Peak observed ~7k.

economics.block_reward: validator rewards from inflation + tx fees

ecosystem.audit_status: Multiple audits; Firedancer (Jump) is independent client

highlights: Highest live observed TPS of major L1s (~3-7k sustained)
Sub-second confirmation, ~13s finality
Lowest fees ($0.00025) of major chains
Firedancer client improves resilience (multi-client)
Strong meme/DeFi/payments product-market fit 2024-2025
weaknesses: 65k TPS figure includes vote tx; real user TPS is far lower
MEV via Jito blocks ~50%+ of stake (centralization risk)

chains.name: Sui

performance.tps_note: 297k claimed peak; observed sustained ~500-1500 on mainnet 2024.

crypto.signature_scheme: Ed25519 (default), ECDSA secp256k1, ECDSA secp256r1, BLS12-381, zkLogin

economics.block_reward: stake rewards from gas + storage fund

ecosystem.audit_status: OtterSec, Zellic audits; Move type system enforces resource safety

highlights: Object-centric model enables parallel execution without sharding
Sub-second finality via Mysticeti DAG-BFT
Move language: linear types prevent double-spend at type level
zkLogin enables Web2 OAuth -> wallet (no seed phrase)
Sponsored transactions (gas paid by app)
weaknesses: 297k TPS is testnet peak, not sustained mainnet
Tokenomics: large team/investor unlocks ongoing through 2030

chains.name: Optimism (OP Mainnet)

performance.tps_note: Block gas limit allows ~2k TPS; observed ~10-20 on mainnet. OP Stack chains aggregated higher.

architecture.consensus: Optimistic Rollup (single sequencer + 7-day fraud proof window)

economics.block_reward: no native issuance; sequencer earns fees

ecosystem.audit_status: OP Stack has fault proofs live (Jun 2024); audited by Sigma Prime, Trail of Bits, Spearbit

highlights: OP Stack: standard for many L2s (Base, Mode, Zora, Worldchain)
Superchain vision: shared sequencer + cross-rollup messaging
Fault proofs live (permissionless validation)
EIP-4844 blobs cut fees ~10x in 2024
Full EVM equivalence (no contract changes from L1)
weaknesses: Centralized sequencer (single OP Labs node) — censorship risk
L2 fees still spike with L1 base fee surges

chains.name: Base (Coinbase L2)

performance.tps_note: Block gas limit ~50M; observed ~30-100 TPS sustained. Highest TPS L2 by user activity 2024-2025.

architecture.consensus: Optimistic Rollup (OP Stack) — sequencer + 7-day fraud proof window

economics.max_supply: no native token (uses ETH for gas)
economics.block_reward: no native issuance; sequencer earns priority fees
economics.tx_cost_note: Reduced ~10x by EIP-4844 blobs (Mar 2024). Often $0.005-$0.02 typical.

ecosystem.audit_status: OP Stack audited by Sigma Prime, Trail of Bits, Spearbit. Fault proofs live since 2024.

highlights: Top L2 by TVL ($11B+) and DAU (1.8M+) in 2024-2025
Backed by Coinbase — US-regulated, easy onramp from coinbase.com to Base
Built on OP Stack (Superchain ecosystem with Optimism, Mode, Zora, Worldchain)
EIP-4844 blobs cut fees ~10x in 2024 (typical tx <$0.02)
Full EVM equivalence — all Ethereum contracts/tooling work unchanged
Strong consumer apps (Farcaster, friend.tech legacy, Aerodrome)
weaknesses: Centralized sequencer (Coinbase only) — single point of censorship/failure
L2 fees still spike when Ethereum L1 base fee surges
Coinbase company risk: SEC actions, regulatory changes affect Base
No native token — value capture different from L1s
sources: C:/Kits work/limaje de programare/3_DESKTOP_APPS/aweb3/deployments-bridge-sepolia.json (bridge supports 84532 Base Sepolia)

chains.name: Cardano

performance.tps_note: Real throughput limited by block size (88KB) and 20s slots. Hydra L2 aimed at ~1M TPS theoretical.

crypto.signature_scheme: Ed25519 + KES (forward-secure for slot leaders) + VRF

economics.block_reward: staking rewards from reserve + tx fees

ecosystem.audit_status: Peer-reviewed Ouroboros papers; Plutus formally specified in Coq/Agda

highlights: Most academically rigorous: peer-reviewed consensus papers
Extended UTxO model (concurrency by design)
Native multi-asset (no token-contract bug class)
Hydra L2 for high-throughput payments/dApps
Treasury (~$1B+ ADA) funds Catalyst grants
weaknesses: Smaller DeFi ecosystem; Hydra adoption limited
Plutus dev experience friction vs. Solidity

chains.name: Polygon PoS

performance.tps_note: ~30-100 sustained TPS observed. Theoretical from Heimdall+Bor capacity.

ecosystem.audit_status: Multiple audits; bridge has had vulnerabilities patched

highlights: Cheap (~$0.02) full-EVM compatibility
Large user base (~350k DAU); Reddit/Starbucks/Disney NFT deployments
Polygon CDK + AggLayer for ZK rollup ecosystem
POL token enables multi-chain validation
Mature dev tooling (Hardhat/Foundry/Truffle work as-is)
weaknesses: Sidechain (not real L2) — own validator set, weaker security than rollups
Validator set ~100, more centralized than Ethereum

chains.name: Avalanche (C-Chain)

performance.tps_note: C-Chain ~10-50 TPS. Subnets/L1s can independently achieve thousands.

architecture.vm: EVM (C-Chain), AvalancheVM (X-Chain DAG), custom on subnets
architecture.sharding_note: Subnets = independent L1s sharing validator security

highlights: Sub-second probabilistic finality via Snowman
Subnets/L1s allow custom VMs + tokenomics with shared security
EVM-compatible C-Chain
AVAX deflationary (all gas burnt)
Permissioned subnets enable enterprise/RWA use
weaknesses: Subnet adoption mixed (DFK, Beam succeeded; many died)

chains.name: LCX Liberty Chain

performance.tps_note: EVM L1, no public production benchmark. Testnet active (chain 76847801). Mainnet timeline unclear as of 2025-Q1.

architecture.consensus: PoA (Proof-of-Authority) — Clique-based, validator set curated by LCX AG

crypto.pq_status: Inherits Ethereum crypto stack — not PQ

economics.block_reward: validator gas fees only (PoA, no inflation)
economics.tx_cost_note: Low fees — PoA + low traffic. LCX token used for gas + payments + DEX fees on LCX Exchange

ecosystem.audit_status: LCX AG (Liechtenstein-regulated VASP); audits not publicly disclosed
ecosystem.regulatory: Liechtenstein FMA registered. EU MiCA-compliant entity (LCX AG). One of few regulated crypto exchanges in EU.

highlights: Operated by LCX AG — one of few EU/Liechtenstein-regulated crypto entities (FMA license, MiCA-aligned)
EVM-compatible — all Ethereum tooling works (Hardhat, Foundry, MetaMask)
Native bridge to Sepolia (Ethereum) — see OmniBusBridgeRelay deployment in aweb3 project
Low fees + 2s blocks suit payment + DEX use cases
Used as settlement layer for LCX Exchange order book
weaknesses: PoA (centralized validator set) — security depends on LCX AG curation, not decentralized
Mainnet not live yet (testnet only as of 2025-Q1)
Ecosystem small (DAU ~2k); no major dApps deployed beyond LCX-internal
TVL ~$5M (estimate) — orders of magnitude below mature L1s

chains.name: Aptos

performance.tps_note: Block-STM enables parallelism. Mainnet sustained ~1-3k TPS.

ecosystem.audit_status: OtterSec, Zellic audits; Move Prover for formal verification

highlights: Block-STM optimistic parallel execution
Move Prover: formal verification toolkit
Sub-second finality (~1s)
Keyless accounts (OIDC/zkLogin equivalent) live
Strong infra investor backing (a16z, Jump, Tiger)
weaknesses: 160k TPS is testnet peak, not sustained

chains.name: NEAR Protocol

performance.tps_note: Nightshade sharding aims for 100k. Current 4-6 shards, ~50-200 TPS observed.

economics.max_supply: 1,000,000,000 NEAR initial (5%/y inflation, 70% to validators, 30% burn)

ecosystem.audit_status: Audits by HALBORN, Trail of Bits

highlights: Nightshade dynamic sharding (6 shards, scaling with demand)
Human-readable account names (alice.near)
Chain Abstraction / Chain Signatures (cross-chain UX)
Sub-2s finality with deterministic fork choice
High DAU (Sweat Economy, Hot Wallet)
weaknesses: 100k TPS is theoretical with full sharding deployed
Most user activity is meme/gaming, low value
Aurora (EVM) competes for attention with native NEAR

chains.name: Cosmos Hub (ATOM)

performance.tps_note: Tendermint/CometBFT can do ~thousands; Cosmos Hub itself low traffic (~30 TPS sustained).

architecture.vm: CosmWasm (WASM smart contracts) + Cosmos SDK modules
architecture.sharding_note: Each Cosmos chain is its own zone; IBC connects them

highlights: Instant finality (single block, ~6s)
IBC: industrial-strength cross-chain protocol (>110 chains)
Cosmos SDK enables custom L1s in days
ICS (Interchain Security): rent-a-validator-set model
CometBFT used by Binance Chain, Injective, Sei, Celestia, etc.
weaknesses: Hub itself has low TVL/users vs. Cosmos zones
BFT requires 2/3+ honest -> halts under partition

chains.name: Polkadot

performance.tps_note: Theoretical with full parachain set; relay chain modest. Async backing target ~1k TPS per parachain.

architecture.vm: WASM (Substrate runtimes); parachains can have any VM

crypto.pq_status: BEEFY light-client uses ECDSA; PQ research in progress (no production)

economics.max_supply: unlimited (target ~10% annual issuance, dynamic by stake ratio)

ecosystem.audit_status: SR Labs, Halborn, Trail of Bits; Substrate widely audited

highlights: Shared security: parachains rent validation from relay chain
XCM: native cross-chain messaging
Substrate framework powers Kusama, Moonbeam, Astar, Acala
Async backing rolled out (improves throughput per chain)
Forkless runtime upgrades via on-chain governance
weaknesses: Parachain auctions DOA: many slots empty/unused
Complex UX (multiple wallets/chains, no L1 unified token)

chains.name: QRL (Quantum Resistant Ledger)

performance.tps_note: Hash-based sigs are large (~3KB), limiting throughput. Real load very low.

architecture.consensus: PoS (Proof-of-Stake) — migrated from PoW in 2024 (Zond / QRL 2.0)
architecture.vm: EVM (post-Zond upgrade) — with PQ signature wrappers

crypto.signature_scheme: XMSS (eXtended Merkle Signature Scheme, RFC 8391) — hash-based, stateful
crypto.pq_status: First production blockchain with hash-based signatures since 2018

ecosystem.audit_status: X1 Five Bears audit (XMSS impl); minimal external review

highlights: First live PQ blockchain (XMSS hash-based, NIST-approved)
Stateful sigs are well-understood (not new math like lattices)
Zond upgrade adds EVM compatibility for dApps
Hash-based crypto has minimal cryptographic assumptions
weaknesses: XMSS signatures are 2-4KB (vs. 64B ECDSA) -> bloat + slow
Stateful signing: address can sign N times then is unusable (UX risk)
Migration to Zond was multi-year and disruptive

chains.name: QANplatform (QANX)

performance.tps_note: Mainnet not live as of 2025-Q1; 1.6k claimed in testnet docs.

architecture.vm: QVM (multi-language: Rust, Go, JS, C++ via wrappers)

crypto.pq_status: Algorithm choice aligned with NIST PQC standards; mainnet still pending

ecosystem.mainnet_status: TestNet only (2025-Q1); QANX is ERC-20 placeholder on Ethereum
ecosystem.audit_status: Internal only; no public formal verification

highlights: Lattice-based PQ via Dilithium (NIST FIPS 204 standard)
Multi-language smart contracts (no Solidity-only)
EU-based team (Hungary), GDPR-friendly positioning
Lower-latency L1 design (~3s blocks)
weaknesses: No live mainnet — token QANX is just an ERC-20 placeholder

chains.name: IOTA (Stardust / Rebased)

performance.tps_note: Rebased (MoveVM, 2025 mainnet) targets higher; legacy Stardust/Chrysalis was ~10-100 TPS.

architecture.consensus: Mysticeti (DAG-BFT, post-Rebased; same family as Sui)
architecture.vm: MoveVM (post-Rebased upgrade); legacy was UTXO Stardust

crypto.pq_status: Originally used Winternitz one-time sigs (PQ); abandoned for Ed25519 post-Chrysalis. PQ on roadmap, not live.

economics.tx_cost_note: Was zero-fee in legacy Tangle; Rebased introduces gas fees

ecosystem.mainnet_status: Rebased L1 launched May 2025; legacy Stardust deprecated
ecosystem.audit_status: Multiple audits over years; major redesigns (Coordicide, Chrysalis, Rebased)

highlights: Pioneered feeless DAG (Tangle) — historic, now superseded
Real-world IoT/DLT pilots (EU regulatory engagement)
Rebased adopts MoveVM + Mysticeti (Sui-family tech)
Token holders received airdrop on Rebased migration
weaknesses: PQ Winternitz signatures were ABANDONED (current chain is Ed25519, NOT PQ)
Multiple ground-up rewrites destroyed dev continuity
